pattern: A sorted-then-rotated array has at most one place where an element exceeds its successor (counting the wraparound).
hints: If you walk the array cyclically, a fully sorted-and-rotated array drops from a larger value to a smaller one at most once.
Count the indices where the current element is strictly greater than the next, treating the last-to-first wrap as one of the pairs.
The array is valid exactly when that drop count is zero or one.

edge_inputs.description: two drops means not a single rotation
duplicates at the wrap that stay non-decreasing

stress.description: construct a genuine sorted-and-rotated array roughly half the time, else a random one
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    base = sorted(rng.randrange(1, 101) for _ in range(n))
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        r = rng.randrange(n)
        return [base[r:] + base[:r]]
    return [[rng.randrange(1, 101) for _ in range(n)]]
